name: Gasco Reservoir and Torrelodones Watchtower - circular from Las Matas
name_original: Presa del Gasco y la Atalaya de Torrelodones – circular desde Las Matas
region_intro: Hiking around Las Rozas De Madrid offers diverse natural landscapes, with a significant portion integrated into the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The area features rolling hills, lush green spaces, and Mediterranean scrubland, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Trails often follow riverbanks, pass through dense forests, and include historical features like the El Gasco Dam. The region's proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama also contributes to its diverse natural features. Best hiking trails in Las Rozas De Madrid The most popular hiking route is Gasco Reservoir and Torrelodones Watchtower - circular from Las Matas, an 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 4 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and a historical watchtower. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Gasco Dam – Canal del Guadarrama loop from Las Matas, a difficult 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path featuring the historic dam and canal. Local hikers also love the Stepped Canal – Fluvial Canal of the Park loop from Ciudad del Fútbol de Las Rozas, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through green spaces and reconstructed river sections, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Las Rozas De Madrid is defined by rolling hills, Mediterranean scrubland, and historical dam features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Las Rozas De Madrid are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Las Rozas De Madrid's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: There are over 60 hiking routes available in Las Rozas De Madrid, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: The terrain around Las Rozas De Madrid is diverse, featuring rolling hills, Mediterranean scrubland, and dense forests. Many trails follow riverbanks, pass through holm oak and juniper groves, and include historical features like the El Gasco Dam. The area is part of the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, offering varied ecosystems.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 25 easy routes in Las Rozas De Madrid. For a relaxed experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14885321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stepped Canal – Fluvial Canal of the Park loop from Ciudad del Fútbol de Las Rozas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.5 miles (7.3 km) and takes less than 2 hours. Parque El Cantizal also offers spacious green areas ideal for family outings.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: Many routes in the area are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957803587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gasco Reservoir and Torrelodones Watchtower - circular from Las Matas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail offering views of the reservoir and a historical watchtower.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore various natural features and landmarks. The area is rich with the Manzanares River basin, dehesas with ash and holm oak trees, and parts of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Notable man-made features include the El Gasco Dam and the Torrelodones Watchtower. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/868170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Solana Lookout Tower&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2818819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Madroñal Summit and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature water bodies like reservoirs or dams?
A: Yes, several routes highlight water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6928887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gasco Dam – Canal del Guadarrama loop from Las Matas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path featuring the historic dam and canal. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1456505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de la Hoz Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What is the best time to hike in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal due to milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer mornings or late afternoons can also be suitable, though it's advisable to avoid the midday heat. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower elevation trails.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: Many trails in the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares and surrounding areas are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage. Always keep your dog under control, especially in protected areas, and be mindful of local wildlife and other hikers.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Las Rozas De Madrid accessible by public transport?
A: Some trailheads in Las Rozas De Madrid are accessible via public transport, particularly those closer to urban centers or main roads. For routes starting from places like Las Matas or Ciudad del Fútbol de Las Rozas, public bus services often provide connections. It's best to check local bus routes and schedules for specific starting points.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads and parks in and around Las Rozas De Madrid offer designated parking areas. For example, routes starting from Las Matas or Ciudad del Fútbol de Las Rozas typically have parking facilities nearby, making it convenient for those traveling by car.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the opportunities to explore historical sites like the El Gasco Dam. The variety of routes, from easy strolls to more challenging treks, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views in the Las Rozas area?
A: For stunning panoramic vistas, adventurous trekkers can head to Cerro de San Pedro. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2818819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Madroñal Summit and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent viewpoints across the picturesque landscapes of the region.
tips: The initial 10 kilometers of the Guadarrama Canal, between the Gasco dam and Las Rozas, have remains of the original work, several aqueducts to channel the water from the streams and the dam wall, a valuable set of Spanish civil engineering from the century XVIII.

The El Gasco dam, also called Peña Strait or Carlos III Dam, is one of the most important civil engineering projects in 18th century Spain. It is located above the Guadarrama River gorge, in an area currently protected by the regional park of the middle course of the Guadarrama River and its surroundings.

Icon of the city of Torrelodones and appreciated by many when passing through the A-6 motorway. The current tower is a restoration, while very little remains of the original. The construction dates back to the 10th century when the area was under the command of the Caliphate of Cordoba and after being abandoned for centuries, it was rebuilt in 1930.
